message:
feat(ftui-extras): add Console abstraction for styled output (bd-1ho.2)

Add Console module providing rich-style output helpers adapted to
ftui's one-writer constraints:

- Segment-first design (works with structured text + style)
- Width-aware wrapping (word/character modes)
- Style stack for nested styling with CSS-like cascading
- Capture mode for testing
- ConsoleSink abstraction (capture or writer output)

16 unit tests + 3 doc tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1ho","title":"Rich Core Infrastructure (rich_rust port)","description":"# Rich Core Infrastructure (reference extraction, feature-gated)

## Purpose
This epic tracks optional infrastructure ideas inspired by `rich_rust`.

The plan keeps rich_rust’s best abstractions (Segments / measurement protocol / markup parser), but de-scopes “port everything” into feature-gated extras.

## Reference Source
- `legacy_reference_library_code/rich_rust/`

## Plan Alignment
From the plan (Chapter 1.4 / 1.5 / Phase 7):
- Segments + measurement protocol are valuable
- export-friendly representations are valuable
- “widget zoo / markdown / syntax / forms” are extras

We treat this epic as Phase 7 **extras infrastructure** unless/until a specific piece is required by the agent harness.

## Tracked Issues (this epic’s children)
- `bd-1ho.2` Console abstraction (optional ergonomic output helpers)
- `bd-1ho.5` Live display system (optional “live updating” view patterns)
- `bd-1ho.6` Filesize formatter (small utility; optional)
- `bd-1ho.7` Logging integration (optional formatting/bridge on top of `tracing`)
- `bd-1ho.8` Extra test suite coverage for the above

Related (rich-derived but implemented elsewhere in the plan tree):
- `bd-1ho.1` Segment system (Cow<str>)
- `bd-1ho.3` TextMeasurement protocol

## Non-Goals
- Locking the kernel behind a large “rich port”
- Introducing new, parallel APIs that duplicate ftui core

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Each child issue clearly states how it integrates with ftui (crate/module + feature flag).
- [ ] Optional infrastructure cannot violate one-writer rule or inline-mode invariants.
- [ ] Tests cover behavior and failure modes; logs are actionable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:02:34.577032474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T00:23:42.778697422Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ho","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:59:07.678702154Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":13,"issue_id":"bd-1ho","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ho.1","title":"Implement Segment system (Cow<str> styled text units)","description":"# Implement Segment system (Cow<str> styled text units)

## Background
Plan Appendix D.1 describes a Segment system inspired by rich_rust.

A Segment is the atomic unit of styled text that can be:
- cheaply borrowed (`Cow<str>`) for string literals / static content
- split at **cell positions** (not byte positions) for correct wrapping

Segments are a bridge:
- higher-level text/layout systems produce Segments
- widgets ultimately paint to `Frame`/`Buffer` using measured/split segments

## Core types
### Segment
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub struct Segment<'a> {
    pub text: Cow<'a, str>,
    pub style: Option<Style>,
    pub control: Option<SmallVec<[ControlCode; 2]>>, // optional; stack-alloc common case
}
```

### Control codes (optional)
Segments may carry control codes for non-textual actions (rare; typically not needed in the kernel).
If we keep them, they must be constrained and documented.

## Key APIs
- `cell_length()` (display width)
- `split_at_cell(cell_pos)` (critical for wrapping with wide chars/ZWJ)
- helpers to split/join lines of segments

## Design constraints
- Splitting MUST respect Unicode scalar boundaries; ideally grapheme boundaries.
- Control segments must not consume width.
- ASCII width fast-path is a later optimization (see bd-v6y) and must not change correctness.

## Tests
- split_at_cell correctness for:
  - ASCII
  - CJK wide chars
  - emoji/ZWJ sequences
- cell_length matches `unicode_width` for the same input
- line splitting preserves styles and does not drop content

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Segment type exists with `Cow<str>` storage.
- [ ] `cell_length` and `split_at_cell` APIs exist and are tested.
- [ ] Segment line-splitting/joining helpers exist.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Functional Requirements
- [ ] Create segment with owned String
- [ ] Create segment with borrowed &str (Cow optimization)
- [ ] Create control segments (newline, home, bell)
- [ ] Calculate cell length accounting for:
  - ASCII characters (1 cell each)
  - Wide characters (2 cells each)
  - Zero-width characters (0 cells)
  - Control segments (0 cells)
- [ ] Split segment at grapheme boundary
- [ ] Strip styles preserving text
- [ ] Join multiple segments
- [ ] Split segments on newlines into Lines
- [ ] Join Lines back into segments

### Zero-Copy Requirements
- [ ] String literals never allocate
- [ ] Short strings use Cow::Borrowed when possible
- [ ] Split returns borrowed segments when feasible
- [ ] Concatenation minimizes copies

### Performance Requirements
- [ ] Cell length calculation: < 50ns for typical segment
- [ ] Split operation: O(1) for ASCII content
- [ ] Join operation: O(n) where n = total chars

### Logging Requirements
- [ ] tracing::trace! for segment creation (debug builds)
- [ ] tracing::warn! for unexpectedly long segments (> 10KB)

### Test Coverage
- [ ] Unit tests for all segment operations
- [ ] Property test: split-join is identity
- [ ] Property test: cell length >= 0
- [ ] Corpus test: Unicode edge cases (ZWJ, RTL, combining)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:02:46.925829242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T17:50:25.418469745Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T17:50:25.418450880Z","close_reason":"Implemented Segment system with Cow<str> storage, cell_length(), split_at_cell(), SegmentLine, SegmentLines, and line splitting utilities - 40 tests passing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.1","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:14:50.969799531Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.1","depends_on_id":"bd-10x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:18.847212946Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T00:43:16.700422525Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1ho.2","title":"Implement Console abstraction","description":"# Implement Console abstraction (Segment-first, one-writer safe)

## Objective
Provide an optional, ergonomic “console output” helper inspired by rich-style APIs, but adapted to ftui’s constraints:
- **Segment-first** (structured text + style)
- **one-writer safe** (no ad-hoc terminal writes)

This is primarily useful for:
- formatting tool output / logs into wrapped lines
- producing captured output for tests
- generating exportable text streams

## Reference (for ideas)
- `legacy_reference_library_code/rich_rust/src/console.rs`

## Scope
- A `Console` type that:
  - accepts `Segment` streams (and/or `Renderable` → `Segment`)
  - performs width-aware wrapping
  - maintains an internal style stack for nested styling
  - can operate in “record/capture” mode for tests

## Output Policy (Critical)
- The Console must not bypass ftui’s output routing.
- Any “print” API must target:
  - an in-memory buffer, OR
  - a `LogSink`/writer abstraction that is already sanitized and one-writer compliant.

## Dependencies / Integration
- Depends on Segment system and markup parsing.
- Should integrate with ftui-style and ftui-text measurement.

## Tests Required
- Unit tests for wrapping behavior (including wide/combining chars via existing width utilities).
- Tests for style stack push/pop correctness.
- Capture-mode tests that assert deterministic output.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `Console` exists with width-aware wrapping and a style stack.
- [ ] Capture/record mode exists and is used in tests.
- [ ] No API writes directly to stdout/stderr; output routing is explicit.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Functional Requirements
- [ ] Width detection from terminal or fallback to 80
- [ ] Explicit width override
- [ ] Print plain strings with automatic wrapping
- [ ] Print styled text with style application
- [ ] Print any Renderable with measurement and rendering
- [ ] Style stack: push/pop for nested styling
- [ ] Markup string parsing and rendering
- [ ] Recording mode for test capture

### Color System Requirements
- [ ] Auto-detect color capability from environment
- [ ] NO_COLOR environment variable respected
- [ ] Force color override option
- [ ] Color downgrade for limited terminals

### Output Requirements
- [ ] All output respects current width
- [ ] Long lines wrapped at word boundaries
- [ ] Style reset at end of output
- [ ] No raw escape codes in output (sanitized)

### Logging Requirements
- [ ] tracing::debug! for width detection
- [ ] tracing::debug! for color system detection
- [ ] tracing::trace! for style stack operations
- [ ] tracing::info! for recording start/stop

### Test Coverage
- [ ] Unit tests for width handling
- [ ] Unit tests for style stack
- [ ] Unit tests for color system detection
- [ ] Integration tests with renderables
- [ ] Capture/assertion tests for output verification","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:02:54.445310573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:33:47.145020486Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.2","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:20.442951992Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:02:54.445310573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:19.913985950Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T00:26:14.137790103Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3mo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:20.973683948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":12,"issue_id":"bd-1ho.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1ho.3","title":"Implement TextMeasurement protocol (Appendix D.3)","description":"# Implement TextMeasurement protocol (Appendix D.3)

## Background
Plan Appendix D.3 defines a simple measurement protocol used for layout negotiation.

Key insight:
- measurement is *bounds* (minimum + maximum), not a single width
- bounds compose with simple algebra (union vs stack)

This supports deterministic layout without ad-hoc heuristics.

## Core type (from plan)
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
pub struct TextMeasurement {
    pub minimum: usize,
    pub maximum: usize,
}

impl TextMeasurement {
    pub const ZERO: Self = Self { minimum: 0, maximum: 0 };

    /// Union: take max of both bounds (for side-by-side layout)
    pub fn union(self, other: Self) -> Self {
        Self { minimum: self.minimum.max(other.minimum), maximum: self.maximum.max(other.maximum) }
    }

    /// Stack: add both bounds (for vertical stacking)
    pub fn stack(self, other: Self) -> Self {
        Self { minimum: self.minimum + other.minimum, maximum: self.maximum + other.maximum }
    }

    pub fn clamp(self, min_width: Option<usize>, max_width: Option<usize>) -> Self {
        let mut result = self;
        if let Some(min_w) = min_width {
            result.minimum = result.minimum.max(min_w);
            result.maximum = result.maximum.max(min_w);
        }
        if let Some(max_w) = max_width {
            result.minimum = result.minimum.min(max_w);
            result.maximum = result.maximum.min(max_w);
        }
        result
    }
}
```

## Integration points
- `ftui-text`: compute measurements for Text/Segments
- `ftui-layout`: use measurements when allocating space for widgets

## Tests
- union/stack/clamp behave exactly as defined
- clamp invariants:
  - minimum <= maximum
  - clamps preserve ordering

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `TextMeasurement` exists with union/stack/clamp.
- [ ] Unit tests cover composition laws.
- [ ] Adopted by layout/text measurement code paths (call sites tracked in follow-up issues).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:03:01.763981159Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:44:05.268581429Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:44:05.268517559Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.3","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:20:15.236621599Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T00:43:15.109855603Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ho.4","title":"Implement border/box drawing character sets (Appendix D.5)","description":"# Implement border/box drawing character sets (Appendix D.5)

## Background
Plan Phase 4 includes: "Border presets and custom".

Appendix D.5 references a border system inspired by charmed_rust and rich_rust:
- reusable border presets (rounded, square, double, heavy, ascii)
- used by Panel/Block widgets
- should degrade gracefully when Unicode box drawing is undesirable

## Scope
- Define a border/box character set type (name it `BorderChars` / `BorderSet` etc; avoid `Box` name collisions).
- Provide presets:
  - Rounded
  - Square
  - Double
  - Heavy
  - ASCII fallback
- Include T-intersections and cross characters for tables where needed.

## Design notes
- Border characters are from Unicode Box Drawing (U+2500..U+257F).
- ASCII fallback uses `+`, `-`, `|`.
- This is a *data* module; actual drawing happens in widgets (`Block`, `Panel`, `Table`).

## Tests
- Presets contain expected characters (sanity checks)
- ASCII fallback is complete and consistent

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Border character set type exists.
- [ ] Presets exist and are documented.
- [ ] Used by Block/Panel widgets (follow-up dependencies tracked).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:03:07.697787474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T19:10:49.349187282Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T19:10:49.349167564Z","close_reason":"Added BorderSet tees/cross + presets (ASCII, Square, Rounded, Double, Heavy) with tests; updated BorderType variants","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.4","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:20:49.805784770Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ho.5","title":"Implement Live display system","description":"# Implement Live display system (optional)

## Objective
Provide an optional “live updating” display helper (progress/status refresh) for cases where we want transient output in a normal terminal stream.

This is **not** the core ftui rendering model (full-buffer diff + presenter). It’s an optional utility inspired by rich-style “live” output.

## Reference (for ideas)
- `legacy_reference_library_code/rich_rust/src/live.rs`

## Design Constraints (ftui-specific)
- Must respect the **one-writer rule**: any redirection of output must be explicit.
- Must not compromise inline-mode invariants when used alongside the harness.
- Must have bounded refresh behavior (no busy looping).

## Scope
- A `Live` controller that:
  - renders a `Renderable`/`Segment`-like view repeatedly
  - supports a configurable refresh cadence
  - cleans up cursor/modes on stop/drop
  - provides vertical overflow strategies (crop/ellipsis/visible)

## Integration
- Reuse Presenter encoding for cursor save/restore, clears, etc.
- Reuse Console abstraction for rendering content into segments/lines.

## Tests Required
- Unit tests for overflow strategies and refresh cadence logic.
- PTY integration tests for cleanup on stop/drop and on panic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Live rendering can start/stop cleanly and is idempotent.
- [ ] Refresh cadence is bounded and testable.
- [ ] PTY tests verify cursor/modes are restored after Live stops.
- [ ] Output routing is explicit (no hidden writes that violate one-writer rule).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:03:18.946085437Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T00:26:40.308488926Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.5","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:23.110285746Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:03:18.946085437Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:22.582300790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3ky.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:23.633608498Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":11,"issue_id":"bd-1ho.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1nn0","title":"Add recovery mechanism with configurable attempts","description":"# Add recovery mechanism with configurable attempts

## Background
Part of Widget Error Boundaries (bd-1euu). After a widget fails, we should try to recover when conditions change (e.g., model updates).

## Technical Design

```rust
/// Configuration for error boundary behavior
#[derive(Clone)]
pub struct ErrorBoundaryConfig {
    /// Maximum recovery attempts before giving up
    pub max_recovery_attempts: u32,
    
    /// Delay between recovery attempts (in frames)
    pub recovery_delay_frames: u32,
    
    /// Whether to log errors
    pub log_errors: bool,
    
    /// Custom fallback widget factory
    pub fallback_factory: Option<Box<dyn Fn(&CapturedError) -> Box<dyn Widget>>>,
    
    /// Whether to try recovery on model update
    pub recover_on_model_update: bool,
}

impl Default for ErrorBoundaryConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            max_recovery_attempts: 3,
            recovery_delay_frames: 5,
            log_errors: true,
            fallback_factory: None,
            recover_on_model_update: true,
        }
    }
}

impl<W: Widget> ErrorBoundary<W> {
    /// Attempt to recover from error state
    /// Call this when model changes or after delay
    pub fn try_recover(&mut self) -> bool {
        match &mut self.state {
            ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(error) => {
                if self.config.max_recovery_attempts > 0 {
                    tracing::info!(
                        widget = self.name,
                        attempt = 1,
                        "Starting recovery attempt"
                    );
                    self.state = ErrorBoundaryState::Recovering {
                        attempts: 1,
                        last_error: error.clone(),
                    };
                    true
                } else {
                    false
                }
            }
            ErrorBoundaryState::Recovering { attempts, last_error } => {
                if *attempts < self.config.max_recovery_attempts {
                    *attempts += 1;
                    tracing::info!(
                        widget = self.name,
                        attempt = *attempts,
                        max = self.config.max_recovery_attempts,
                        "Continuing recovery attempt"
                    );
                    true
                } else {
                    // Give up permanently
                    self.state = ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(last_error.clone());
                    tracing::warn!(
                        widget = self.name,
                        "Recovery failed after max attempts"
                    );
                    false
                }
            }
            ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy => true,
        }
    }
    
    /// Reset to healthy state (force recovery)
    pub fn reset(&mut self) {
        self.state = ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy;
        tracing::info!(widget = self.name, "Error boundary reset");
    }
    
    /// Signal that model has updated (may trigger recovery)
    pub fn on_model_update(&mut self) {
        if self.config.recover_on_model_update && self.is_failed() {
            self.try_recover();
        }
    }
    
    /// Get recovery status
    pub fn recovery_status(&self) -> RecoveryStatus {
        match &self.state {
            ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy => RecoveryStatus::Healthy,
            ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(_) => RecoveryStatus::Failed,
            ErrorBoundaryState::Recovering { attempts, .. } => {
                RecoveryStatus::Recovering {
                    attempts: *attempts,
                    max_attempts: self.config.max_recovery_attempts,
                }
            }
        }
    }
}

/// Recovery status for external monitoring
#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum RecoveryStatus {
    Healthy,
    Failed,
    Recovering { attempts: u32, max_attempts: u32 },
}
```

## Recovery Triggers
1. **Manual**: Call try_recover() explicitly
2. **Model update**: Automatic when config.recover_on_model_update
3. **Force reset**: Call reset() to immediately clear error

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ErrorBoundaryConfig with all options
- [ ] try_recover() increments attempts
- [ ] Max attempts enforced
- [ ] reset() forces healthy state
- [ ] on_model_update() triggers recovery
- [ ] RecoveryStatus for monitoring","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:18:18.791599230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T19:29:50.166964103Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T19:29:50.166938815Z","close_reason":"Recovery mechanism already implemented in ErrorBoundaryState with try_recover(max_attempts), Recovering state, attempts counter, and configurable max_recovery_attempts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nn0","depends_on_id":"bd-1fpo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:19:54.161041282Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1oz","title":"Implement LRU width cache for text measurement","description":"# Implement LRU Width Cache for Text Measurement

## Background
Plan Section 1.4 and Appendix D.3 explicitly mention the "LRU width cache" from rich_rust as a key optimization. Text width measurement is a hot path (called for every render) and must be cached.

## Why LRU Cache?
- Unicode width calculation is expensive (grapheme segmentation + width lookup)
- Same text strings appear repeatedly (labels, status text, etc.)
- Cache hit rate expected to be high for typical UIs
- Prevents O(n) per-frame width recalculation

## Design
```rust
/// Width cache with LRU eviction
pub struct WidthCache {
    cache: LinkedHashMap<u64, usize>,  // hash -> width
    capacity: usize,
}

impl WidthCache {
    pub fn new(capacity: usize) -> Self {
        Self {
            cache: LinkedHashMap::with_capacity(capacity),
            capacity,
        }
    }

    /// Get cached width or compute and cache
    pub fn get_or_compute(&mut self, text: &str, compute: impl FnOnce() -> usize) -> usize {
        let hash = hash_text(text);
        
        if let Some(&width) = self.cache.get(&hash) {
            // Move to front (LRU refresh)
            self.cache.get_refresh(&hash);
            return width;
        }
        
        let width = compute();
        
        // Evict if over capacity
        while self.cache.len() >= self.capacity {
            self.cache.pop_front();
        }
        
        self.cache.insert(hash, width);
        width
    }
    
    pub fn clear(&mut self) {
        self.cache.clear();
    }
}

// Use FNV or xxhash for fast hashing
fn hash_text(text: &str) -> u64 {
    use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};
    let mut hasher = FnvHasher::default();
    text.hash(&mut hasher);
    hasher.finish()
}
```

## Integration Points
- `RenderContext` holds a `RefCell<WidthCache>`
- `Text::cell_length()` uses the cache
- `Segment::cell_length()` uses the cache
- Cache is cleared on theme change (fonts can affect width)

## Cache Sizing
- Default: 1024 entries (covers typical UI with headroom)
- Configurable via `WidthCache::new(capacity)`
- Memory footprint: ~32 bytes per entry (hash + width + list pointers)

## Performance Requirements
- Cache hit: < 50ns (hash + lookup)
- Cache miss: < 5µs (grapheme segmentation + width calculation)
- Hash collision handling: rare, just recompute

## Tests Required
- [ ] Cache hits return correct cached value
- [ ] Cache misses compute and cache
- [ ] LRU eviction works correctly
- [ ] Clear() empties cache
- [ ] Thread safety if needed (RefCell for single-threaded)
- [ ] Performance benchmarks

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] LRU cache implemented with configurable capacity
- [ ] Integrated into text measurement hot path
- [ ] Performance benchmarks show significant speedup
- [ ] No correctness regressions in width measurement

## Location

-{"id":"bd-1qb","title":"Implement Image widget with protocol detection","description":"PHASE 7 - EXTRAS: IMAGE WIDGET

## Background
Image widget displays images in terminal using Kitty Graphics Protocol, iTerm2 inline images, or Sixel fallback. Requires protocol detection and graceful degradation.

## Technical Requirements
- Protocol detection:
  - Kitty Graphics Protocol (XTGETTCAP query)
  - iTerm2 inline images (TERM_PROGRAM check)
  - Sixel (DA2 response parsing)
  - Fallback: ASCII/Braille representation
- Image loading via image crate
- Resize/fit modes: Contain, Cover, Stretch, None
- Aspect ratio preservation
- Partial update for animated GIFs
- Memory management for large images

## Design Rationale
Image support is a premium feature gated behind 'image' flag. Protocol detection happens once at startup and caches result. Fallback ensures graceful degradation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Protocol detection and caching
- [ ] Kitty Graphics Protocol implementation
- [ ] iTerm2 inline image implementation
- [ ] Sixel fallback (optional sub-feature)
- [ ] ASCII fallback when no protocol available
- [ ] Resize mode configuration
- [ ] Aspect ratio handling
- [ ] Feature gate: image

## Performance Budget
- Protocol detection: < 50ms (one-time)
- Image encode/transmit: proportional to size

 {"id":"bd-1qpv","title":"Implement ErrorBoundary<W> wrapper widget","description":"# Implement ErrorBoundary<W> wrapper widget

## Background
Part of Widget Error Boundaries (bd-1euu). The core wrapper that catches panics and renders fallback.

## Technical Design

```rust
use std::panic::{catch_unwind, AssertUnwindSafe};

/// Widget that catches panics from inner widget
pub struct ErrorBoundary<W: Widget> {
    /// The wrapped widget
    inner: W,
    
    /// Widget name for error reporting
    name: &'static str,
    
    /// Current state
    state: ErrorBoundaryState,
    
    /// Configuration
    config: ErrorBoundaryConfig,
}

/// Error boundary state machine
enum ErrorBoundaryState {
    /// Widget is healthy
    Healthy,
    
    /// Widget has failed, showing fallback
    Failed(CapturedError),
    
    /// Attempting recovery
    Recovering {
        attempts: u32,
        last_error: CapturedError,
    },
}

impl<W: Widget> ErrorBoundary<W> {
    /// Wrap a widget in an error boundary
    pub fn new(inner: W, name: &'static str) -> Self {
        Self {
            inner,
            name,
            state: ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy,
            config: ErrorBoundaryConfig::default(),
        }
    }
    
    /// Configure max recovery attempts
    pub fn with_max_recovery(mut self, max: u32) -> Self {
        self.config.max_recovery_attempts = max;
        self
    }
    
    /// Configure custom fallback factory
    pub fn with_fallback<F>(mut self, factory: F) -> Self
    where
        F: Fn(&CapturedError) -> Box<dyn Widget> + 'static,
    {
        self.config.fallback_factory = Some(Box::new(factory));
        self
    }
    
    /// Check if in error state
    pub fn is_failed(&self) -> bool {
        matches\!(self.state, ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(_))
    }
    
    /// Get current error if any
    pub fn error(&self) -> Option<&CapturedError> {
        match &self.state {
            ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(e) => Some(e),
            ErrorBoundaryState::Recovering { last_error, .. } => Some(last_error),
            ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy => None,
        }
    }
}

impl<W: Widget> Widget for ErrorBoundary<W> {
    fn render(&mut self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer, budget: &RenderBudget) {
        match &self.state {
            ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy => {
                // Try to render inner widget
                let result = catch_unwind(AssertUnwindSafe(|| {
                    self.inner.render(area, buf, budget)
                }));
                
                if let Err(panic) = result {
                    let error = CapturedError::from_panic(
                        panic,
                        self.name,
                        area,
                        RenderPhase::Render,
                    );
                    self.handle_error(error, area, buf);
                }
            }
            ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(error) => {
                self.render_fallback(error, area, buf);
            }
            ErrorBoundaryState::Recovering { last_error, .. } => {
                // Try render, but have fallback ready
                let result = catch_unwind(AssertUnwindSafe(|| {
                    self.inner.render(area, buf, budget)
                }));
                
                match result {
                    Ok(()) => {
                        // Recovery successful\!
                        self.state = ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy;
                        tracing::info\!(widget = self.name, "Widget recovered");
                    }
                    Err(panic) => {
                        let error = CapturedError::from_panic(
                            panic,
                            self.name,
                            area,
                            RenderPhase::Render,
                        );
                        self.handle_recovery_failure(error, area, buf);
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
    
    fn is_essential(&self) -> bool {
        self.inner.is_essential()
    }
}

impl<W: Widget> ErrorBoundary<W> {
    fn handle_error(&mut self, error: CapturedError, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        if self.config.log_errors {
            tracing::error\!(
                widget = error.widget_name,
                message = %error.message,
                area = ?error.area,
                "Widget panicked"
            );
        }
        
        self.state = ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(error.clone());
        self.render_fallback(&error, area, buf);
    }
    
    fn handle_recovery_failure(&mut self, error: CapturedError, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        if let ErrorBoundaryState::Recovering { attempts, .. } = &mut self.state {
            *attempts += 1;
            if *attempts >= self.config.max_recovery_attempts {
                // Give up, stay in failed state
                self.state = ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(error.clone());
                tracing::warn\!(
                    widget = self.name,
                    attempts = self.config.max_recovery_attempts,
                    "Widget recovery failed, giving up"
                );
            }
        }
        self.render_fallback(&error, area, buf);
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ErrorBoundary<W> wraps any Widget
- [ ] catch_unwind catches panics
- [ ] State machine tracks Healthy/Failed/Recovering
- [ ] Builder pattern for configuration
- [ ] is_essential() delegates to inner","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:18:17.335714425Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T19:28:24.699522774Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T19:28:24.699494631Z","close_reason":"ErrorBoundary<W> wrapper widget already implemented as part of bd-1euu in ftui-widgets/src/error_boundary.rs with StatefulWidget impl, panic capture via catch_unwind, recovery mechanism, and fallback rendering","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1qpv","depends_on_id":"bd-b8m5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:19:04.775737401Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
